title: 264. Aktiepodden synar vd-rockader och Elektarapporten
summary_md:
# 264. Aktiepodden synar vd-rockader och Elektarapporten

**Feed**: aktiepodden  
**Published**: 2026-05-29  
**Market Sentiment**: Mixed

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

Episode 264 of Aktiepodden contrasts a rising rate market signalling caution against equity indices at all-time highs, driven by a highly concentrated AI/semiconductor rally and US AI capex that is largely rate-insensitive while Europe stays rate-sensitive. The hosts dissect three CEO changes (Nolato, Hexpol, Kinnevik) — concluding leadership swaps are hard to trade except where a clear strategic turnaround is needed (notably Kinnevik) — and debate Elekta's weak report, where order intake fell but margins improved on cost cuts; both end seeing a cheap stock that still lacks the growth trigger. They close by flagging Sivers' stretched valuation.

---

## Universe Stocks

### 🟠 EKTA-B — Elekta | **WATCH** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Mixed | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 50%

Weak Q order intake (-22%) but improved gross margin and adjusted EBIT from cost cuts; cheap valuation (~1.2x order book) but growth trigger still absent; heavily shorted (12-13%).

#### Reasoning

Cost-savings program lifted margins as expected, but the market has long doubted Elekta's organic growth and US competitiveness vs Varian. Both hosts agree the real trigger is top-line growth and uptake of the new product generation, not margins. Valuation is low and could offer upside if growth returns, but weak market confidence drives big swings; high short interest is a caution flag. Net: monitor for a growth inflection before committing.

#### Sell Triggers

- if organic/order growth fails to recover in coming quarters
- if uptake of new product generation stays weak
- if US market-share gains do not materialize
- if short interest keeps rising

> *"Kan man bara få lite ordning på, nu har man ordning på kostnaderna, kan man bara få lite, lite tillväxt så kanske det finns lite uppsida härifrån."*

---

### ⛔ SIVE — Sivers Semiconductors | **AVOID** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Negative | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Valued ~22bn SEK, about 7x its ~450m USD opportunity pipeline vs Elekta at 1.2x order book; hosts say even if all opportunities are realized the valuation is sky-high.

#### Reasoning

Hosts frame Sivers as an AI/photonics FOMO rocket whose 'opportunity pipeline' metric is vague and far softer than booked orders. At ~7x that speculative pipeline versus Elekta near book value, they judge the valuation extreme even under optimistic conversion assumptions, repeatedly joking the enthusiasm is largely about not owning it. Directionally cautious on the rich multiple.

#### Sell Triggers

- if opportunity pipeline fails to convert to real orders/revenue
- if AI/semiconductor sentiment cools
- if growth disappoints relative to priced-in expectations

> *"Även om alla de här möjligheterna blir verklighet så är det fortfarande en skyhög värdering."*

---

### 🟢 MCOV-B — Medicover | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Liked for exposure to Poland and broader East/Central European growth markets that have matched the Nasdaq Composite over recent years; Poland overtook Japan in GDP per capita.

#### Reasoning

Hosts highlight Medicover as a way to ride a structural 'Eastern Europe train' alongside the AI rally, citing Poland's strong economic dynamism and rising GDP per capita. They view exposure to these higher-growth, less industrially traditional markets as an attractive and underappreciated theme worth building positions around.

#### Sell Triggers

- if Polish/CEE macro growth stalls
- if regional currency or political risk escalates

> *"Vi gillar Medicover för exponeringen mot Polen som är en ekonomi som går som tåget."*

---

### 🟠 KINV-B — Kinnevik | **WATCH** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

New CEO Helena Saxon (ex-Investor CFO, Goldman background) marks a shift from unprofitable growth investing toward a traditional investment-company model; market regained confidence and the stock rose on the news.

#### Reasoning

Hosts see the CEO change as a genuine, well-timed trigger after years of criticized strategy and capital allocation (selling profitable Tele2/MTG/Millicom while funding loss-makers). Saxon's investment-focused profile fits the needed pivot, and low expectations plus positive share reaction make it a credible turnaround to watch, tempered only by her two heavy board commitments.

#### Sell Triggers

- if the new strategy/capital allocation fails to deliver
- if board commitments distract from the CEO role
- if NAV discount fails to narrow

> *"Så där känns det ju som att ett vd-byte verkligen är en trigger för kursen, tycker jag."*

---

### 🟠 HPOL-B — Hexpol | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 45%

CEO change (Claes Dahlberg out, Henrik Elmin from Atlas Copco in) viewed as a good move; hosts like the polymer business though the stock has lagged and input costs are tough.

#### Reasoning

Hosts call it an exciting company and a good CEO change, with strategy set and a new leader brought in for stronger, faster execution; the fact that Elmin is also courted for the Nibe chairmanship signals his quality. But the share has not impressed recently and raw-material cost pressure persists, so it is a watch rather than a conviction buy.

#### Sell Triggers

- if input/raw-material cost pressure worsens
- if execution under the new CEO disappoints
- if end-market demand stays weak

> *"Hexpol nämnde vi här i början. Det känns som ett bra vd-byte. Jag gillar Hexpol."*

---

### 🟠 BEIA-B — Beijer Alma | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 45%

Transitioning toward a serial-acquirer model; acting CEO Johnny Alvarsson's Indutrade background seen as a strong profile match for that journey.

#### Reasoning

Hosts use Beijer Alma as an example where the leader's profile fits the strategic direction: Alvarsson's experience at highly successful serial acquirer Indutrade suits Beijer Alma's evolution into a serial acquirer. Viewed positively as a good match, but it is an illustrative aside rather than a strong, fully-argued buy thesis.

#### Sell Triggers

- if the serial-acquisition strategy fails to gain traction
- if a permanent CEO shifts direction
- if acquisition returns on capital disappoint

> *"Den profilen passar ju väldigt bra i den typen av resa som Ber Alma är på väg in i."*

---
